opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Running an agent at the OS level introduces real context-switching overhead when the model interrupts the host process, which can noticeably degrade interactive responsiveness. I've seen teams underestimate the complexity of maintaining consistent tool-use state across agent restarts—the simplest approach is to serialize the working-memory graph but that quickly becomes unwieldy.
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building multi-provider LLM routing systems, tool orchestration frameworks, and self-improving agent loops that deal with state persistence, resource contention, and sandboxing—directly relevant to OS-level agent architecture.
